description: Focus 'ko' points the listener to the constituent carrying contrastive or new information. It highlights one alternative and keeps the others available; 'li' (RESTR) marks exclusion. Because focus has its own particle, it remains visible in writing and need not depend on louder stress.
articulatory_notes: The back of the tongue closes against the soft palate for 'k' and releases into rounded true-mid 'o'. Full stress falls on this one syllable.
sound_symbolism: The complete velar closure gives 'ko' a clear edge before the rounded vowel. That short boundary can cue the item brought into focus.
usage_notes: The particle 'ko' stands immediately before the focused word. It follows plural or numeral material and precedes ordinary degree marking in the Slot 2 cline. The superlative is the fixed pair 'mo ko'; the complete marker still precedes its target.

examples.translation: I am the one who sees you.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: A speaker can put contrast into 'ko' instead of sharper volume. A correction can therefore locate its point at an ordinary speaking level.
